name: God
nameMeaning: The one true God; Creator and covenant Lord
summary: God creates, judges, redeems, and keeps covenant throughout the biblical story.

name: Adam
summary: Adam is the first man, created in God’s image and placed in Eden.

name: Eve
summary: Eve is the first woman and mother of all living.

name: Cain
summary: Cain is Adam and Eve’s son who murdered Abel and was sent away from the Lord’s presence.

name: Abel
summary: Abel is Adam and Eve’s son whose offering was accepted and whose blood cried out after Cain killed him.

name: Seth
summary: Seth is the son given to Adam and Eve after Abel’s death and part of the line leading to Noah.

name: Enoch
summary: Enoch walked with God and was taken by God according to Genesis 5.

name: Methuselah
summary: Methuselah is remembered in Genesis 5 as the longest-lived man in the genealogy from Adam to Noah.

name: Noah
summary: Noah obeyed God, built the ark, and became the covenant head of humanity after the flood.

name: Shem
summary: Shem is one of Noah’s sons and ancestor of the line that leads toward Abraham.

name: Ham
summary: Ham is one of Noah’s sons and is named among those preserved through the flood.

name: Japheth
summary: Japheth is one of Noah’s sons and is named among the families preserved through the flood.

name: Abraham
summary: Abraham receives God’s covenant promises of land, offspring, and blessing to the nations.

name: Sarah
summary: Sarah is Abraham’s wife and Isaac’s mother, through whom God preserved the promised line.

name: Hagar
summary: Hagar is Sarah’s Egyptian servant and Ishmael’s mother, seen by God in the wilderness.

name: Ishmael
summary: Ishmael is Abraham and Hagar’s son, preserved by God and promised a great nation.

name: Lot
summary: Lot is Abraham’s nephew who settled near Sodom and was rescued before its destruction.

name: Melchizedek
summary: Melchizedek is king of Salem and priest of God Most High who blessed Abram.

name: Isaac
summary: Isaac is the promised son of Abraham and Sarah through whom the covenant line continues.

name: Rebekah
summary: Rebekah becomes Isaac’s wife and mother of Jacob and Esau.

name: Jacob
nameMeaning: יַעֲקֹב (Ya aqov), heel-holder/supplanter; Israel means God strives
summary: Jacob, later named Israel, fathers the family that becomes the twelve tribes.

name: Esau
summary: Esau is Jacob’s twin brother who sold his birthright and lost Isaac’s blessing.

name: Leah
summary: Leah is Jacob’s wife and mother of several sons, including Judah.

name: Rachel
summary: Rachel is Jacob’s beloved wife and mother of Joseph and Benjamin.

name: Joseph
summary: Joseph is sold into Egypt and later preserves his family during famine.

name: Judah
summary: Judah is Jacob and Leah’s son whose tribe later carries royal significance.

name: Benjamin
nameMeaning: בִּנְיָמִין (Binyamin), son of the right hand
summary: Benjamin is Jacob and Rachel’s youngest son and part of the family that went to Egypt.

name: Potiphar
summary: Potiphar is the Egyptian official who bought Joseph as a slave.

name: Moses
summary: Moses leads Israel out of Egypt, receives the law, and shepherds the people through the wilderness.

name: Aaron
summary: Aaron is Moses’ brother, Israel’s first high priest, and Moses’ spokesman before Pharaoh.

name: Miriam
summary: Miriam is Moses and Aaron’s sister, a prophet who led worship after the sea crossing.

name: Jethro
summary: Jethro is Moses’ father-in-law and priest of Midian who counseled Moses.

name: Joshua
summary: Joshua succeeds Moses and leads Israel into Canaan.

name: Caleb
summary: Caleb is the faithful spy who trusted God and later received Hebron.

name: Rahab
summary: Rahab protected Israel’s spies in Jericho and was spared when the city fell.

name: Balaam
summary: Balaam was hired to curse Israel but spoke blessings under God’s constraint.

name: Deborah
summary: Deborah judged Israel and summoned Barak in the conflict with Sisera.

name: Barak
summary: Barak led Israel’s forces at Deborah’s command against Sisera.

name: Gideon
summary: Gideon was called by God to deliver Israel from Midian.

name: Jephthah
summary: Jephthah led Gilead against the Ammonites and is remembered for a tragic vow.

name: Samson
summary: Samson was a Nazirite judge whose strength was used against the Philistines.

name: Delilah
summary: Delilah betrayed Samson to the Philistines after discovering the secret of his strength.

name: Ruth
nameMeaning: רוּת (Rut), meaning uncertain, often associated with friend/companion
summary: Ruth is a Moabite woman whose loyalty to Naomi led her to Bethlehem and Boaz.

name: Naomi
summary: Naomi is Ruth’s mother-in-law who returned from Moab to Bethlehem.

name: Boaz
summary: Boaz redeemed Naomi’s family line and married Ruth.

name: Eli
summary: Eli was priest and judge at Shiloh when Samuel was called.

name: Hannah
summary: Hannah prayed for a son and dedicated Samuel to the Lord.

name: Samuel
nameMeaning: שְׁמוּאֵל (Shemu el), heard by God/Name of God
summary: Samuel was called by God as a prophet and judged Israel at the transition toward monarchy.

name: Saul
summary: Saul was Israel's first king, anointed by Samuel and later rejected for disobedience.

name: Jonathan
summary: Jonathan was Saul's son and David's covenant friend.

name: David
summary: David was the shepherd from Bethlehem who became king over all Israel and received the Davidic covenant.

name: Goliath
summary: Goliath was the Philistine champion defeated by David in the Valley of Elah.

name: Bathsheba
summary: Bathsheba was the wife of David and mother of Solomon.

name: Nathan
summary: Nathan was the prophet who delivered the Davidic covenant promise and confronted David's sin.

name: Absalom
summary: Absalom was David's son who led a rebellion against his father.

name: Joab
summary: Joab was David's powerful army commander.

name: Solomon
summary: Solomon was David's son, known for wisdom, wealth, and building the temple.

name: Rehoboam
summary: Rehoboam was Solomon's son whose harsh rule helped divide the kingdom.

name: Jeroboam
summary: Jeroboam became the first king of the northern kingdom of Israel.

name: Ahab
summary: Ahab was a northern king whose reign was marked by Baal worship and conflict with Elijah.

name: Jezebel
summary: Jezebel was Ahab's queen and a major promoter of Baal worship in Israel.

name: Elijah
summary: Elijah was a prophet who confronted Ahab, Jezebel, and Baal worship in Israel.

name: Elisha
summary: Elisha succeeded Elijah and ministered in the northern kingdom through signs and prophetic counsel.

name: Naaman
summary: Naaman was an Aramean commander healed through Elisha's prophetic word.

name: Jonah
summary: Jonah was the prophet sent to warn Nineveh.

name: Amos
summary: Amos warned Israel about injustice, false worship, and coming judgment.

name: Hosea
summary: Hosea warned the northern kingdom through a message of covenant betrayal and restoring mercy.

name: Isaiah
summary: Isaiah ministered in Judah during the Assyrian crisis and proclaimed judgment, comfort, and servant hope.

name: Hezekiah
summary: Hezekiah was a king of Judah who sought the Lord during Sennacherib's invasion.

name: Sennacherib
summary: Sennacherib was the Assyrian king who invaded Judah in 701 BC.

name: Josiah
summary: Josiah was a reforming king of Judah who renewed the covenant after the law was found.

name: Jeremiah
summary: Jeremiah warned Judah before Jerusalem fell and promised a new covenant.

name: Nebuchadnezzar
summary: Nebuchadnezzar was the Babylonian king who conquered Jerusalem and ruled the exiles.

name: Daniel
summary: Daniel was a Judean exile who served in foreign courts and interpreted dreams and visions.

name: Shadrach
summary: Shadrach was one of Daniel's faithful friends in Babylon.

name: Meshach
summary: Meshach was one of Daniel's faithful friends in Babylon.

name: Abednego
summary: Abednego was one of Daniel's faithful friends in Babylon.

name: Ezekiel
summary: Ezekiel was a priest-prophet who ministered among the exiles in Babylon.

name: Belshazzar
summary: Belshazzar was the Babylonian ruler in Daniel 5 when Babylon fell.

name: Darius the Mede
summary: Darius the Mede is the ruler named in Daniel 6 during the transition after Babylon's fall.

name: Cyrus
summary: Cyrus was the Persian king whose decree allowed the exiles to return and rebuild the temple.

name: Zerubbabel
summary: Zerubbabel led early returnees and oversaw the rebuilding of the temple.

name: Esther
summary: Esther was the Jewish queen who interceded to save her people in Persia.

name: Mordecai
summary: Mordecai was Esther's cousin and guardian who helped expose Haman's plot.

name: Ahasuerus
summary: Ahasuerus was the Persian king in Esther, commonly identified with Xerxes I.

name: Ezra
summary: Ezra was a priest and scribe who taught the law in postexilic Jerusalem.

name: Nehemiah
summary: Nehemiah led the rebuilding of Jerusalem's walls under Persian authority.

name: Haggai
summary: Haggai encouraged the returned exiles to finish rebuilding the temple.

name: Zechariah
summary: Zechariah encouraged the restored community with visions of renewal and future hope.

name: Malachi
summary: Malachi rebuked postexilic covenant unfaithfulness and promised a coming messenger.
